metadata.title: SEC filings, primary-source pass - the circular-funding thesis in the companies' OWN risk factors and financials (EDGAR)
metadata.frame: Zero-trust on secondary press: re-anchor the proven financial core to PRIMARY SEC filings (10-K/10-Q/8-K/S-1/6-K/13F on EDGAR). The point - the firms' own mandatory disclosures describe the circular concentration and leverage the SCC/Z3/TLA+ models formalize. Customer-concentration and going-concern-adjacent risk language is the 'circular core' stated in legalese; the variable-rate debt load is the cascade fragility.
metadata.WARNING: Filings corroborate, they do NOT change the proofs (the proofs already ran on the consolidated graph). Figures are as-reported by the filer; 'as-reported' is itself an object of scrutiny (non-GAAP add-backs, segment definitions). Primary links given so any number is checkable on EDGAR.
metadata.method: SEC EDGAR (sec.gov/Archives) + filer 10-K/10-Q text, cross-read with StockTitan/IndexBox extractions; verify against the filing itself.

key_disclosures: CUSTOMER CONCENTRATION: ~67% of 2025 revenue from Microsoft; backlog anchored to a handful of counterparties - OpenAI (~$22.4B), Meta (~$35.2B implied), Anthropic, Jane Street.
DEBT: total >$21B (from <$8B in 2024); large portion VARIABLE-rate averaging ~11%; Q4-2025 interest expense $388M (~1/3 of quarterly revenue).
Net loss ~$1.2B (FY2025); 43 data-center sites, >850 MW active power; continued dependence on financing + stable power markets.
Risk factors explicitly flag dependence on a few customers, contract renegotiation/cancellation, and customers building in-house compute.
filings.thesis_link: This is the cascade node (TLA+): a neocloud whose revenue is ~2/3 one customer and whose ~$21B variable-rate debt eats ~1/3 of revenue in interest. The filing's own concentration + leverage language IS the OpenAI->CoreWeave->Oracle fragility the model traces.

key_disclosures: Customer concentration disclosed: Direct Customer A ~12%, B ~11%, C ~11% of FY2025 revenue (each >=10%).
Subsequent analyses: top-4 direct customers ~61% of Q3-FY2026 sales; concentration grew from ~36% to ~61% in a year.
Risk factor: 'A significant amount of our revenue stems from a limited number of partners and distributors... revenue could be adversely affected if we lose... any of these end customers.'
filings.thesis_link: NVIDIA's own filing confirms the demand side of the loop is a handful of buyers (the same hyperscalers/neoclouds it invests in). Rising 36%->61% concentration is the SCC tightening - corroborates the vendor-financing self-funding metric (build_graph: ~10% funded / ~56% headline).

key_disclosures: ~27% of OpenAI on an as-converted basis, accounted for under the EQUITY METHOD.
Total funding commitment $13B; $11.6B FUNDED as of Sep 30 2025.

OpenAI's losses flow through 'other income (expense), net' as Microsoft's share; Q1-FY2026 other income showed a ~$683M loss 'primarily reflecting net recognized losses on equity method investments, including OpenAI.'
External reporting infers Microsoft's recognized share implies an OpenAI quarterly loss on the order of ~$11.5B+ (i.e., OpenAI burning at scale).
filings_added_2026_06.thesis_link: PRIMARY confirmation of the Z3 result (OpenAI needs >=$1.03T external; insolvent at zero inflow): Microsoft's own filing books its 27% share of OpenAI's losses, and the implied OpenAI burn (~$11.5B/qtr) is the cash the loop must keep raising. Reconciles the $13B cash-funded vs ~$135B marked-stake gap (cross_review RECONCILED).

key_disclosures: Remaining Performance Obligations (RPO) ~$523.3B as of Nov 30 2025 (up ~359% YoY in a prior quarter to ~$455B) - a backlog dominated by OpenAI/Stargate-linked cloud capacity.
OCI revenue guided on a hockey-stick: ~$18B -> $32B -> $73B -> $114B -> $144B over five years.
>=$72B of data-center PARTNER DEBT across 3 packages serving Stargate: $16.3B (Michigan, Saline Twp - largest single-facility tech debt ever), ~$38B (Texas/Wisconsin), ~$18B (New Mexico).
PIMCO anchored ~$10B of the $16.3B Michigan bond after US BANKS RETREATED from the deal.
Oracle FY2026 capex ~$50B (>2x prior year); sold $18B of public bonds in a single day (Sep 2025) to fund OpenAI data centers.
filings_added_2026_06.thesis_link: The clearest primary-source picture of the loop's funding mechanics: a $523B backlog that is largely ONE circular counterparty (OpenAI/Stargate), financed by >=$72B partner debt + $50B capex + $18B bonds - with PIMCO stepping in when banks pulled back (private credit as the marginal lender, ties macro-cre-privatecredit). This IS 'solvent only while capital keeps flowing,' in Oracle's filings.

pull_queue: Amazon/Google 10-K: capex run-rate + 'AI infrastructure' commitments; Amazon's Anthropic carrying value + equity-method treatment.
SpaceX/SPCX S-1/S-1/A (CIK 1181412): revenue mix + the Google equity stake + cancelable compute deals (already in fin-spacex-spcx).
13F cross-holdings: hyperscaler stakes in labs/neoclouds (the equity edges of the SCC) - to be pulled from EDGAR 13F filings.

edges.notes: CoreWeave 10-K: ~67% of FY2025 revenue from Microsoft - primary-source confirmation of the concentration the SCC encodes (a hyperscaler funding a neocloud that buys NVIDIA).
Total debt >$21B (from <$8B 2024), ~11% variable; Q4 interest $388M (~1/3 of revenue). The leverage that makes the cascade node fragile.
PIMCO anchored ~$10B of Oracle's $16.3B Michigan data-center bond after US banks retreated - private credit as the marginal lender for the Stargate build (ties macro-cre-privatecredit).
Oracle's ~$523B RPO backlog is dominated by OpenAI/Stargate cloud capacity - the demand side of the loop, financed by >=$72B partner debt + $50B capex.

assessment: Re-anchoring to primary EDGAR filings strengthens the core finding without changing a proof: the firms DISCLOSE the circular concentration and leverage themselves. CoreWeave's FY2025 10-K states ~67% revenue from Microsoft, >$21B debt (from <$8B) at ~11% variable with $388M quarterly interest (~1/3 of revenue), and a $1.2B net loss - the exact fragility the TLA+ cascade and the 'insolvent at zero inflow' Z3 result describe, in the filer's own risk language. NVIDIA's 10-K discloses customer concentration (A/B/C each >=10%; ~36%->61% in a year) - the demand side of the loop, corroborating the vendor-financing self-funding metric. The honest caveat: 'as reported' is itself contestable (non-GAAP add-backs, segment definitions, RPO timing) - the same scrutiny the data-integrity block applies to government statistics applies to corporate filings. A pull-queue lists the next primary documents (Microsoft/Oracle/Amazon/Google capex + equity-method, SpaceX S-1, 13F cross-holdings). Overlay/corroboration layer; the proofs already stand on the consolidated graph.
